payload.title: AI code review workflow idea
payload.description: test

payload.title: E2E test scenario - shaping item
E2E test reject item
E2E test intake capture observation
payload.description: discovered work item

payload.title: E2E test retro finding
payload.description: test handoff finding

payload.title: compute-verification-method accepts list-valued change_type — stop crashing on multi-type stories
Fail loud on empty/unrecognized change_type and reconcile index from story frontmatter
Refresh README skill counts after sprint merge
Add exclusion lists and verification recipes to grep-gate / out-of-repo ACs
payload.description: The sprint's only HIGH false positive (AC8 wiki-ingest) plus AC6/AC9/AC11 false-FAIL re-adjudications all trace to absolute grep conditions with no carve-outs or to ACs whose effects live outside the repo (Obsidian KB vault). Found independently by review, coordination, and both story lenses. Cost a full Phase 4d investigation cycle and an annotation fix round.
suggested_ac: Grep-gate ACs must include an explicit exclusion list (cross-repo paths, _bmad/ framework internals, .claude/skills/ templates, historical/audit-table records) at authoring time
ACs with out-of-repo effects must specify an in-repo evidence artifact (ingest manifest, commit note, or log path) as the verification target
Outsider Test / planning AVFL fails any grep-gate AC lacking an exclusion list and any out-of-repo AC lacking an in-repo evidence artifact
When the contract strips an unverifiable clause, the corresponding story AC is rescoped in the same pass (no deferral to 'warning')

payload.title: Make missing-skill handling uniformly halt-and-flag; register create-story agent type
payload.description: momentum:create-story was unregistered; planning silently hand-wrote stories ('Do NOT use the Skill tool'), bypassing change-type classification, EDD/TDD injection, and per-story AVFL — while the same orchestrator halted-and-flagged correctly at Phase 4b. Name-resolution drift (momentum-tools, un-namespaced architecture-guard) compounds it. Workflow-fidelity violation.
suggested_ac: momentum:create-story and any agent-type referenced by sprint-planning is registered/resolvable in the environment
On any dispatch failure for a delegated skill/agent, the orchestrator halts and flags to the developer — never silently substitutes a hand-written equivalent
A naming-consistency pass over workflow files resolves every command/skill/agent-type reference to its registered name (full plugin path, correct namespace)

payload.title: Scope retro audit-extracts by sprint/story affiliation, not file mtime
payload.description: ~70% of the agent corpus and ~42% of user messages are out-of-window; the lone team-message is another sprint's retro reviewer. Every downstream lens receives heavy noise and risks misattributing findings. Found by execution, review, and coordination lenses.
suggested_ac: Extraction keys rows by sprint/story affiliation (session-to-sprint mapping), not modification date alone
The --before bound excludes the next sprint's conduct
The team-messages extractor matches actual SendMessage tool_use blocks, not substring '%SendMessage%' in assistant content
user-messages extraction de-dupes slash-command skill expansions and excludes automated task-notification frames

payload.title: Formalize the developer-led guided walkthrough as a pre-close phase
payload.description: The human-initiated walkthrough caught a P0 happy-path break (campaign creation broken on both platforms), spec gaps, and an architecture-level product error (5-phase wizard contradicting 'only a campaign name required') that automated validation missed entirely.
suggested_ac: sprint-dev includes a pre-close phase that produces step-by-step expected-behavior instructions and invites a developer walkthrough before the end-gate
The end-gate does not propose close while a human validation loop is in progress
Happy-path E2E coverage exists for primary user flows so the walkthrough is a confirmation, not the only safety net

merged_observations.title: Defer session close while human validation is mid-flight
merged_observations.description: An agent proposed wrapping up while a developer-led validation activity was mid-flight ('No don't close the session, we want to continue with our walkthrough'). Closing momentum should defer to in-progress human validation loops.

payload.title: Uniform post-fix re-validation by a validator pass; generate gate reports from logs
payload.description: Three AVFL instances used three different fix-verification disciplines; the self-certified instance let a wrong dismissal (FIX-5) through, caught only because a later gate happened to re-check. Gate exit reports drifted from fixer logs (reported 6 vs actual 5 MEDIUM); dispatch prompts were stream-of-consciousness.
suggested_ac: Every AVFL instance verifies applied fixes via a dedicated validator pass — never by the fixer's or orchestrator's own assertion
Gate exit reports are generated from the fixer log, not hand-assembled
Fixer dispatch prompts contain cleaned, non-contradictory step instructions

merged_observations.title: AVFL re-validation re-runs full validator sets against full files
merged_observations.description: DESIGN.md consumed 405 validator turns across multiple AVFL rounds because later rounds re-run full validator sets against the full file. Diff-scoped re-validation rounds would preserve rigor at a fraction of the cost. Related to the risk-tier action item but distinct (re-validation round scoping, n

payload.title: Tie story-status transition to the merge event
payload.description: Dev declared MERGE-READY without transitioning status; QA happened to catch 'status is still ready-for-dev'. The story file still reads ready-for-dev today with all 16 checkboxes unchecked and empty Dev Agent Record while index.json says done.
suggested_ac: The story-status transition to done is triggered by the merge event (conductor/sprint-manager responsibility), not left implicit in dev
Either the dev workflow enforces story-file bookkeeping (status, checkboxes, Dev Agent Record) on completion, or the duplicated status field is removed from the story template
LOW-severity review findings are not silently dropped from the targeted-fix queue

merged_observations.title: Dev completion signal inconsistently emitted across stories
merged_observations.description: The heaviest dev agent (191 turns) ended on a wiki-ingest subtask report with no visible MERGE-READY/file_list block, while its sibling ended with the canonical block. Either the signal contract is inconsistently emitted or the extractor truncates final messages. One of two stories lacks a machine-r

payload.title: Risk-tier the validation pipeline by sprint size/class
payload.description: A docs-only, no-production-code sprint received the full multi-stage stack at fixed depth, with at least three stages re-verifying the identical 24 ACs against the filesystem (~1.6-2.0:1 validation:dev ratio). Real defects were caught, but roughly half the turn cost is avoidable on docs-only sprints.
suggested_ac: The pipeline classifies sprint risk (e.g. docs-only / config-only / production-code) and scales validation depth accordingly
AVFL re-validation rounds scope to the diff of applied fixes rather than re-running full validator sets against the full file
A single stage owns AC-vs-filesystem verification; downstream stages consume its result rather than re-deriving it

payload.title: Reduce intake per-spawn overhead via batching; standing guideline for multi-line python
payload.description: Two single-item intake spawns burned 33 turns each vs ~4.75 turns/item batched (~7x). Six inline-python one-liner failures from nested quotes/regex under zsh.
suggested_ac: Multiple backlog stubs are created in a single batched intake spawn rather than one spawn per item
A standing guideline directs agents to write temp script files or heredocs for multi-line python instead of python3 -c one-liners

payload.title: Validator depth variance up to 11x within a lens is invisible in spawn metadata
payload.description: Same-lens validators differed up to 11x in depth (88 vs 8 turns); the 2-tool-call passes read the file once and judged from prompt-supplied focus areas. If the shallow half is an intentional adversary role, the distinction is invisible in spawn metadata so audits/consolidators cannot weight confidence; if unintentional, cross-check confidence is overstated. Surface the adversary-vs-enumerator role distinction in spawn metadata.

failure_diagnosis.root_cause: role distinction not recorded in spawn metadata

payload.title: Orchestrator compacts at nearly every phase boundary — latent context-loss risk
payload.description: The conduct session hit 'ran out of context' 3 times; planning twice (9 compactions in the window). Cross-phase coordination survived only via auto-compaction summaries. It held because the sprint was small and phase state lives in .momentum files, but this is the failure mode where a pending fix list or review finding silently drops from the summary. Investigate durable phase-state handoff independent of compaction.

payload.title: Recurring human re-orientation cost across compactions
payload.description: 9 compaction continuations; the human repeatedly paid re-orientation cost ('OK where were we?', 'What were you doing prior to this?') and threads (e.g. the holistic claude.ai/design discussion) risked being dropped across compactions. Long-running workflows exhausted context mid-flow.

payload.title: High-cost dev agent bundled a separable wiki-ingest sub-workflow
payload.description: The 688 KB / 191-turn dev agent bundled a directory migration plus a full wiki-ingest pipeline, concentrating cost and context-window pressure in one session. The ingest step was separable work. Investigate splitting ingest into its own scoped step/story.

payload.title: Overlapping research spawns fetched the same product corpus twice
payload.description: Two web-research agents (131 combined turns) fetched the same claude.ai/design documentation corpus for distinct questions (broad product understanding vs Tailwind-export). Folding the follow-up question into the first research scope or reusing its fetched corpus would cut combined cost.

payload.title: Verify momentum:code-reviewer is wired into current Phase 4b
payload.description: momentum:code-reviewer was a stub at Phase 4b, halting story 2's review pipeline for ~12h15m; the qa-reviewer substitute lacked adversarial code-review calibration and contributed to the AC-literalist false FAILs. momentum:code-reviewer now exists in plugin 0.28.0 — verify the gap is actually closed and the reviewer is wired into the current sprint Phase 4b.

payload.title: Bake false-positive-avoidance context into the qa-reviewer prompt template
payload.description: After the Phase 4b false positives, an ad-hoc Phase 5 QA prompt gained a 'Critical Context — Avoid False Positives' section (wiki-ingest is a skill not a binary; cross-repo refs are correct) and produced zero overturned findings. The lesson currently lives only in one ad-hoc spawn prompt; bake the verification recipes and known-non-issues into the qa-reviewer prompt template so each sprint does not rediscover it.

payload.title: Directive interpretation drift when the named tool is unavailable
payload.description: Developer said 'Run the bmad code review' (bmad-code-review works on diffs, no PR needed); the agent mapped it to the official claude-plugins code-review plugin (needs a PR), found it unusable, then substituted momentum:qa-reviewer without re-confirming — an explicit directive swapped twice without a check-in. Outcome was acceptable but the mapping should be confirmed when the named tool is unavailable.

payload.title: Hookify-style hard enforcement for cmux pane discipline
payload.description: The cmux pane-discipline rule existed but was violated and corrected twice in one day ('Why do you keep opening new panes when panes already exist?'). Soft prose rules do not reliably constrain pane creation; repeated identical corrections are a classic frustration signal — candidate for hookify-style hard enforcement.

payload.title: Triage UX defects by rendered impact, not described symptom
payload.description: An agent triaged a UX defect by its surface symptom (trailing commas) and missed the actual severity (9 lines of vertical space on mobile). Triage graded by described symptom rather than rendered user impact.

payload.title: Validate interaction-model assumptions at story-creation before multi-story UI flows
payload.description: An entire sprint's 5-phase wizard UI contradicted core product intent ('It should NOT be a wizard in any sense'; 'the ONLY requirement is a campaign name'). The interaction-model assumption surfaced only after build, at walkthrough. Captured as an SDR, but the upstream gap is that multi-story UI flows shipped on an unvalidated interaction model. Surface interaction-model assumptions to the developer at story-creation time.

payload.title: Built-in corpus-wide reference scan as a migration-type sprint close gate
payload.description: Phase 6 verification handed the human a confirm-list containing self-verifiable items, and the migration's completeness check (corpus-wide reference scan) had to be demanded by the human rather than being a built-in close gate. Migration-type sprints should auto-run a corpus-wide reference scan at close; dedup fully automated, consolidation automated-with-visibility (per SDR-010).

payload.title: Make merged-and-deleted the unambiguous terminal sprint state
payload.description: Sprint close left merge/branch state ambiguous enough that the human had to question it ('why wouldn't the sprint be merged? There is no reason to keep a sprint branch around after it's been merged'). sprint-dev close should make merged-and-deleted the unambiguous terminal state.

payload.title: Migrate pre-existing nornspun impetus-core practice stories to the momentum project
payload.description: Follow-up from the sprint-2026-05-25 retro routing correction. The retro skill defaults practice findings to the current project’s impetus-core epic, so nornspun’s backlog accumulated Momentum practice-framework stories that belong in ~/projects/momentum. This retro’s 21 findings were already migrated; this event opens the decision to migrate the PRE-EXISTING ones. DECIDE per story: move to momentum (generic Momentum-framework work) vs keep in nornspun (project-specific practice config). 8 impetus-core stories are clear momentum-framework candidates; 6 ad-hoc/blank-epic stories need individual review (some are nornspun-specific rules/config).

payload.title: Momentum knowledge base buildout
manifesto-builder skill (generate-then-curate)
Conduct — assign finding_id before directed-fix invocation
Conduct — ledger append-site dedup guards
Conduct — resume and rehydration idempotency hardening
Visual HITL gates + presentation-form standard leg
Manifesto format: normative file-pattern / ownership field for deterministic agent resolution
build-guidelines: orchestrated/headless bypass for the role x domain confirmation ask
Live E2E: compose + register + resolve a Gen-2 agent against a real manifest + KB
